Government Agriculture Directed Design User Testing

Farming Disaster Relief Support Tools

Deloitte Digital was engaged by a government department to design a centralised platform and series of tools that support the primary industry when making decisions from their day to day activities, build resilience through preparation and return to normal following a natural disaster.

Client
Government Department
My Role
Senior UX Designer
Delivered via
Deloitte Digital
Deliverables
Research · Flows · Wireframes · Hi-Fi
Impact

Our Impact

Deloitte's Deloitte's contribution to lasting solutions was getting rates of infection for the primary industry to normal following a natural disaster. The approach focused on three goals: give confidence in daily decisions, encourage proactive preparation, and build resilience.

Project outcomes were recognised by the industry for CDNA for outstanding contribution to the preparedness field. Help that does drive primary products and their businesses.

🌾
57,000+ primary producers supported
Connecting farmers to the tools and information they need during disaster events across Australia.
🏆
CDNA recognition
Platform recognised by the CDNA for outstanding contribution to disaster preparedness design.
📊
3 core design goals achieved
Daily decision confidence, proactive preparation tools, and long-term resilience building — all delivered.
Current State

The Problem

A library of farming decision tools exists in the current state, however users are required to input large amounts of data each session that cannot be retained or re-used. The current tools are overly complex and their use-cases are not clearly framed.

Problem 01
Low Tool Literacy
Farmers find it difficult to understand whether a tool is right for their needs. Help text is minimal and on-boarding is not designed for low-frequency, high-stress use.
Problem 02
No Data Persistence
Data cannot be saved or retrieved between sessions. Users re-enter the same information repeatedly — adding unnecessary burden during already stressful events.
Problem 03
Fragmented Ecosystem
Accountability requirements, tool inconsistencies, and unclear use-cases make the current system hard to trust. No single source of truth exists for farmers or administrators.
Design Goals

Objectives

Objective 01
Simplify the experience for farmers with decision-making activities
Re-scope and simplify each tool so farmers can immediately understand what it does and how it applies to their specific situation and land type.
Objective 02
Enable seamless access to tools in disrupted conditions
Design for connectivity edge cases and high-stress use — ensuring the platform performs when and where it is needed most.
Objective 03
Accelerate development within existing platform complexity
Build a componentised design system the engineering team can implement and maintain, reducing rework and accelerating future delivery.
Objective 04
Federate internal development and publishing capability
Establish shared libraries and governance so the department can create and publish new tools independently — without ongoing agency dependency.
How We Worked

Process

1
Phase 1
User Identification & Research
  • User identification and documentation
  • Stakeholder interviews
  • Current state tool audit
  • Journey mapping
  • Priority user flows
2
Phase 2
Information Architecture
  • Account management model
  • Capabilities matrix
  • Sitemap definition
  • Lo-fi wireframes
  • Component specifications
3
Phase 3
High-Fidelity Design
  • Component library
  • Dashboard iteration 1 + testing
  • Dashboard iteration 2
  • Accessibility review (WCAG 2.1)
  • Final design & handoff
Research & Documentation

Priority User Flows & Journey Map

We mapped end-to-end journeys for three primary user types. Account management was the critical finding — users needed to input their land and business context once and have it persist across all tools in the platform. The capabilities matrix defined tool access based on user context.

Priority user flows — account management and cross-tool data persistenceResearch
Platform sitemap — tool hierarchy, access permissions, navigation structureIA
Detailed Design

Wireframes & Specifications

Wireframes moved through three fidelity levels across the engagement — concept sketches, annotated lo-fi, and component-level specifications. Each stage was reviewed with both the government client and the engineering delivery team before progressing.

Dashboard wireframes — layout, data density, query form patternsWireframes
Dashboard

Design Iterations

Iteration 1 Initial dashboard concept — core query form and data output pattern

The dashboard is the centre of the platform — the main hub and primary output surface for all user actions. The first iteration established the core layout: a persistent query form on the left, modular data output panels on the right. Users signing in for the first time expected more guided onboarding, and the query form felt too technical without inline examples.

Iteration 1 — initial query form layout and data module systemIteration 1
Research Insight
First-time users expected guided onboarding before reaching the dashboard — the query form felt intimidating without prior context or setup.
Research Insight
The query form was perceived as too technical. Users struggled with the precision required for location and date range inputs without inline examples.
Design Action
Introduced progressive disclosure in the query form — showing only essential fields by default with optional depth revealed on demand.
Design Action
Redesigned the empty state to guide first-time users through account setup before surfacing the main dashboard, building confidence from the first session.
Iteration 2 Refined for real users — progressive disclosure, onboarding, data confidence

The second iteration responded directly to usability findings. We also introduced a data confidence indicator — a subtle addition that testing confirmed significantly improved user trust in the platform outputs.

Iteration 2 — progressive disclosure, onboarding flow, confidence indicatorsIteration 2
Final Design

Final Design

The final design delivered a fully componentised Figma file with a custom design system, annotated handoff specs, and a pattern library the government department can extend independently. All designs meet WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, validated with a final round of usability testing before handoff.

Final production design — platform dashboard and tool ecosystemFinal Design
Dashboard — final layout across desktop breakpointsFinal Design
